Diabetes Management.key_finding: Spanish-speaking patient outcomes 23% worse due to single bilingual staff member

Hypertension Control.key_finding: Highest ROI in clinic; telehealth pilot showed 31% improvement in elderly adherence

Maternal Health.key_finding: Postpartum depression screening at 63% vs 90% target; single midwife is critical risk

Mental Health.key_finding: 56% over capacity; 6-week wait times; low ROI is a capacity problem not quality problem

need_evidence.fact: 312 ER visits last year — highest preventable visit rate

performance_evidence.fact: ROI of 1.8x — below clinic average of 2.1x
A1C reduction in only 68% of patients vs 70% target
Education class completion rate 54% vs 75% target

need_evidence.fact: 198 ER visits — 22% reduction from prior year

performance_evidence.fact: 74% blood pressure control vs 72% target — exceeds goal
Operating below capacity — 612 patients vs 650 capacity

need_evidence.fact: 820 patients; high-risk pregnancy rate 8.2% vs county 9.1%

performance_evidence.fact: Postpartum depression screening at 63% vs 90% target

resource_evidence.fact: Single midwife covering entire program — critical single point of failure

need_evidence.fact: 389 patients served vs 250 capacity — 56% over capacity
445 ER visits — highest of all programs
Crisis calls increased 38% year over year

performance_evidence.fact: ROI of 0.9x — below break-even due to capacity constraints
6.2 week average wait time vs 2 week target

resource_evidence.fact: Both counselors at critical burnout risk — retention crisis imminent
Budget shortfall of $98,000 — highest in clinic

conflicts.description: Program effectiveness ROI (0.9x) suggests low return on investment, but community need data shows this is the most under-resourced program with 56% overcapacity and 400+ on waitlist.
conflicts.source_a_claim: ROI of 0.9x — below break-even, lowest in clinic
conflicts.source_b_claim: Highest ER visits (445), 56% over capacity, lowest satisfaction (1.3/5), crisis calls up 38%
conflicts.recommended_human_action: Board must decide: ROI calculation does not capture long-term social cost avoidance. Request independent cost-benefit analysis including downstream ER cost savings before using ROI as justification to cut funding.

conflicts.description: Community survey shows Diabetes is a top priority concern, but program outcomes are below target due to a staffing gap — not program design failure.
conflicts.source_a_claim: Diabetes cited as top concern by 6 of 20 survey respondents; language barrier flagged repeatedly
conflicts.source_b_claim: Below target on A1C reduction (68% vs 70%) and class completion (54% vs 75%)
conflicts.recommended_human_action: Investigate whether underperformance is caused by the single bilingual CHW gap before cutting program budget. Outcomes may improve significantly with one additional bilingual hire.

conflicts.description: Hypertension has the highest ROI and spare capacity, but receives less budget than Diabetes despite serving more patients.
conflicts.source_b_claim: Hypertension ROI 2.4x vs Diabetes 1.8x; Hypertension has capacity for 38 more patients
conflicts.recommended_human_action: Review budget allocation logic. Consider reallocating a portion of Diabetes budget to Hypertension expansion while addressing Diabetes language access gap separately.

conflicts.description: Maternal Health has the highest community satisfaction (4.0/5) but is significantly below target on postpartum screening — patients are satisfied but not receiving complete care.
conflicts.source_a_claim: Highest satisfaction score of 4.0/5 across all programs
conflicts.source_b_claim: Postpartum depression screening at 63% vs 90% target — significantly below
conflicts.recommended_human_action: Survey satisfaction may reflect prenatal care quality only. Add postpartum-specific survey questions to next community assessment.

get_health_data.rationale: Epidemiological data with clear methodology; population figures consistent across sources

get_budget_constraints.rationale: Financial data is auditable and objective; shortfall figures verifiable
limitations: Grant funding amounts may shift mid-year

get_survey_results.rationale: 20 responses is a small but directionally useful sample; barriers and concerns consistent with clinical data

get_program_effectiveness.rationale: QI department report with specific metrics and targets; ROI calculations documented
limitations: ROI methodology may undercount social cost avoidance for Mental Health
Annual report may lag current conditions

Diabetes Management.key_uncertainty: Whether bilingual staffing gap is the primary driver of underperformance

Hypertension Control.key_uncertainty: Telehealth expansion cost projections not yet available

Maternal Health.key_uncertainty: Postpartum gap severity — unclear if systemic or staffing issue

Mental Health.key_uncertainty: True ROI when social cost avoidance is included — current 0.9x likely understates value

scores.recommendation_reliability: High confidence in directional recommendations. Mental Health allocation should be treated as conservative minimum given ROI methodology limitations.

Mental Health.primary_rationale: Most urgent need in the clinic. 56% over capacity, 400+ waitlist, both counselors at critical burnout risk. Without investment, program collapse within 12 months is likely. Low ROI (0.9x) is a capacity constraint, not a program quality issue.
evidence_used: get_health_data: 445 ER visits — highest of all programs
get_program_effectiveness: 6.2 week wait time vs 2 week target
get_health_data: Both counselors at CRITICAL burnout risk
Mental Health.human_review_reason: ROI of 0.9x conflicts with capacity data. Board should formally approve reframing Mental Health as a social infrastructure investment before budget increase.

Diabetes Management.primary_rationale: Targeted increase to fund one additional bilingual Community Health Worker. This single hire is projected to close the 23% outcome gap for Spanish-speaking patients and improve class completion from 54% toward the 75% target.
evidence_used: get_survey_results: Language barrier cited by 3 of 6 diabetes respondents
get_health_data: Single bilingual CHW at 105% utilization

Hypertension Control.primary_rationale: Highest ROI program (2.4x) operating below capacity. Modest reduction redirected to Mental Health. Telehealth expansion can proceed with grant funding already secured. Program can maintain quality at reduced budget.
evidence_used: get_program_effectiveness: 612 patients vs 650 capacity — has room

Maternal Health.primary_rationale: Strong grant funding ($195K) offsets reduction. Priority action is hiring a second midwife — funded through grant reallocation rather than base budget increase. Postpartum screening gap to be addressed through workflow redesign.
Maternal Health.human_review_reason: Single midwife remains critical single point of failure. Reduction only advisable if second midwife hire is confirmed through grant funding before budget finalisation.

Community Outreach.primary_rationale: Transportation identified as #1 access barrier across all programs. Small increase to fund mobile clinic pilot days targeting Diabetes and Mental Health populations.
evidence_used: get_survey_results: Transportation cited as top barrier across programs
get_program_effectiveness: Cross-program finding — transportation is #1 barrier

Administration.primary_rationale: Administrative overhead reduced through process efficiency. Savings redirected to direct patient care programs. Note: this assumes shared services consolidation is feasible.
evidence_used: get_budget_constraints: Admin overhead $136,000 total across programs
Administration.human_review_reason: Admin reduction is the largest single change. Requires operational feasibility review before implementation. CFO sign-off required.

allocation.allocation_summary: This allocation prioritises the two most urgent crises: Mental Health capacity collapse and Diabetes language access gaps. Hypertension and Maternal Health modest reductions are offset by strong grant funding positions. The net effect redirects $145,000 toward frontline patient care while maintaining overall budget neutrality.
key_tradeoffs: Mental Health increase funded partly by Hypertension reduction — acceptable given Hypertension's spare capacity and strong ROI
Maternal Health reduction carries risk if second midwife hire is not confirmed through grants
Admin reduction assumes process changes that require leadership commitment to implement
Diabetes increase is targeted (bilingual CHW) rather than broad — ROI should improve measurably within 12 months

implementation_priorities.action: Hire 1 additional bilingual Community Health Worker for Diabetes program
Recruit 1 additional mental health counselor or LCSW
Secure second midwife through grant funding before budget finalisation
Launch mobile clinic pilot days (2x/month) targeting transportation barrier
Expand telehealth for Hypertension elderly patients

flags_for_board: Mental Health ROI methodology understates program value — formal cost-benefit analysis including ER diversion savings recommended before budget vote
Both Mental Health counselors at critical burnout risk — if either leaves, program collapses. Retention bonuses or hiring must be treated as emergency action
Maternal Health single-midwife dependency is a patient safety risk — board should set a hard deadline for second hire
Admin reduction of $100,000 requires CFO operational feasibility review
